mechanism_narrative: A rising month-over-month industrial production index signals expanding real output, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.0% signals sufficient labor market slack to sustain accommodative monetary policy, lowering discount rates and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If periods with UNRATE above 9.0% produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% paired with a 3-month annualized CPI inflation rate below 2.0% suppresses real interest rates and reallocates capital toward equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal triggers and 60-day S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55 percent of instances, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a p-value above 0.05 over any 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals healing labor markets and rising household income expectations, which drives consumption growth and positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If month-over-month PAYEMS increases during post-shock recovery months are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over a 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 2% signals a sharp contraction in consumer demand, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS declines exceeding 2% month-over-month are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of cases over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 500 thousand units signal stabilization in the residential construction sector, supporting construction-related earnings and driving positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If periods with HOUST above 500 thousand are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises month-over-month while retail sales falls month-over-month, the resulting inventory accumulation signals an impending production pullback and profit margin compression, generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month while RSAFS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint condition triggers and 5-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55 percent of episodes over any 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter annualized real GDP growth above 2% signals robust aggregate demand, leading to upward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2% are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of cases over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.
